entry.title: Re: position- business relationship and job relationship...
entry.content:
Hi @viel  - you can try to expose the column "msfSystemRecordStatus" and understand why you get duplicate records. You can ultimately filter results with "N" which stand for normal/final records. SAP has a KBA Note for this same issue - https://me.sap.com/notes/2261877

Hope this helps,

Evangelia

entry.title: Re: ME51N Document Number at Creation
entry.content:
If you mean that after saving, the confirmation message including the created document number doesn't appear, this is probably caused by the mentioned action "The script make some default entries and select the custom tab.". The selection of a tab after saving the document will result in the disappearing success message. 

The same might happen by some tab merging in your flavor - but for ME51N this is not likely. Tab merging goes through all the tabs before displaying the screen, and during that process, a success message will disappear. You can try reproducing this manually without a flavor, when you click through tabs after receiving the confirmation message.

To show the document number, you can grab the success message in the script right after saving but before selecting the custom tab,  and then display it in the status bar or a popup.

If this is not the case, then there may be some other scripting action in the flavor, causing the issue. Difficult to say without taking a look at the actual scenario in your system.

Also, you need to upgrade from that old SP16, since it's out of maintenance/support.

entry.title: Re: Compensation Promotion with Position Management enabled
entry.content:
Hi @catmoye 

Anything is fine except the position itself.

In regards to fields changed by planners in the promotion popup that could become out of sync with the original position defaults values I'm afraid there is no perfect world here and the only solution would be to use a lookup table to prevent these employees from publishing back to EC until they've been checked by an administrator.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from a Custom SAP Fiori Application - My experiences
entry.content:
This blog is to share my experience with integrating SAP UI5 task into SBPA(SAP Build Process Automation ) best way to start integration of SAP UI5 task into Build Process Automation .

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-create-sapui5-task-orderapproval.html

SAP uses predefined templating to create these workflow task as per above learning  journey . 

There is blog post which says one use custom fiori application to trigger . i tried this approach  first . my observations are mostly around this . 

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-discussions/triggering-sap-build-process-automation-workflow-from-a-custom-sap-fiori/m-p/13941417#:~:text=Click%20on%20configure%20button%20and%20add%20input.&text=Now%20our%20api%20trigger%20is,be%20created%20in%20BTP%20cockpit.&text=Ensure%20the%20destination%20is%20correctly,inputs%20required%20for%20your%20workflow .

This allows me to trigger Build Process Workflow but this configuration doesn't allow me to consider current user context also these custom fiori apps cant used in Build Process Automation as UI5 Tasks  . 

Observed a difference how URL is generated when these applications  are deployed to HTML5 repository . Apps which uses workflow templates are MTA Based & Custom fiori application used Manage App Router  , because of these custom fiori apps cant integrated as UI5 tasks in SBPA.  

URL for Workflow based 

URL for Custom Fiori App 

 .

even with Custom fiori application i am not able create workflow when i debug i found that SBPA needs a IRPA api key while making call to API . 

how  to get API key is explained  below .  

1) Navigate to Build Process Control Tower . 

2) Go to Environment where are workflows Deployed '

3). API Keys and add API Key , select the scope and create the key at the end of the you will have KEY which can be used in the UI5 application .

entry.title: Re: Prefer:odata.continue-on-error - Not working in ODATA...
entry.content:
The odata.continue-on-error depends on the backend implementation - it's not related with the IDE you are working on.

But giving the fact that you are using jQuery to call the OData API using a $batch call, you might want to review that decision. It's indeed helpful to show here the payload, in this case including headers.

Also, it's a must to provide further details on how this is implemented in the backend. I understood that you were not able to use the odata.continue-on-error in Postman as well but only in the individual requests.

entry.title: Your AI Agents Are Reshaping Your Workforce. Is Anyone Governing That?
entry.content:
Most AI agent governance conversations focus on IT risk and compliance. Almost none address what agents mean for the people whose work they're changing. Ask most organizations what they're doing to govern their AI agents, and you'll hear about registries, compliance mappings, and access controls. These matter. But they answer questions about systems, not people.

The question that is rarely asked is this: as AI agents take on more of the work your employees currently do, which roles are changing, which skills are becoming less relevant, and where does your organization need to invest in reskilling before the gap becomes a crisis?

AI agents have moved well beyond automating isolated tasks. They now orchestrate workflows, make decisions within defined parameters, and interact with multiple systems in sequence. The workforce implications are no longer hypothetical. They are already arriving, and in most organizations, nobody in a formal governance role is watching.

This is a significant gap, and one that AI governance platforms have almost entirely ignored.

The dominant framing for agent governance has been technical: which agents exist, who owns them, are they compliant, are they performing. These are the right questions for IT governance teams and enterprise architects, and they need to be answered. But they are not the only questions that matter to the organization. HR leaders, workforce planners, and business unit heads are facing a different set of concerns. How is our workforce changing as agents are deployed? Which teams are most affected? Are we investing in the right skills for the environment we're building toward?

Without visibility into the connection between agent deployments and workforce impact, organizations are making significant decisions about their people without the data to support them.

The SAP AI Agent Hub addresses this directly. By connecting agent governance to workforce planning, the platform enables organizations to map each deployed agent to the roles and skills it affects. This gives HR and business leaders a dynamic view of how agent deployments are reshaping workforce needs: which jobs are evolving, which capabilities are in growing demand, where reskilling investment is most urgently needed. These questions become answerable when agent governance is grounded in the people context, not just the IT context.

This is a meaningful differentiator. Connecting agent governance to the IT landscape, business processes, and workforce impact together, in a single platform, is something no other governance approach currently delivers in full. Workforce-focused platforms address the HR dimension well but lack architecture and process context. IT-focused platforms govern agents rigorously but leave the human impact invisible. The SAP AI Agent Hub is designed to close that gap.

Enterprise architects have a role to play here too. EAs already understand system interdependencies at the enterprise level. They know which applications connect to which processes, and which parts of the technology landscape are most sensitive to change. Applying that same lens to the boundary between agents and people is a natural extension of the role. As agents become structural components of how work gets done, the architecture model needs to account for workforce context alongside application and process context.

The organizations that will manage this transition well are not the ones that react when workforce disruption is already visible, when attrition spikes, when skill gaps become critical, when the human cost of ungoverned AI deployment lands on the CHRO's desk. They are the ones who saw it coming in the governance data, because they built a governance model that included their people from the start.

AI agent governance that only asks "what do we have and is it compliant?" is answering half the question. The other half is: what is it doing to our organization, and are we ready for that?

entry.title: Enable Payment Approval Process Before Posting Payment Entry in SAP
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We need an approval process to be implemented before the payment entry is posted in SAP. The process should ensure that:

  • Payment entries are submitted for approval before posting.
  • Only authorized approvers can approve/reject the payment.

  • Approval trail/history should be maintained for audit purposes.

Nandini

entry.title: Introducing SAP AI Agent Hub: Your command center for enterprise-grade AI governance
entry.content:
As AI agents proliferate across platforms and vendors, organizations increasingly lack visibility into which agents exist, what they do, and whether they operate safely. SAP AI Agent Hub is a vendor-agnostic AI governance solution that addresses this directly: it gives enterprise architects, IT administrators, and AI Centers of Excellence a single, structured system of record for all AI assets in the organization, regardless of where they were built or which platform they run on. It covers AI agents, large language models (LLMs), and MCP servers across your entire IT landscape. 

SAP AI Agent Hub is available for Business AI Platform customers who get Joule Studio and/ or AI Units, and as part of SAP LeanIX, SAP's enterprise architecture management solution. 

Discover and inventory your AI assets 

SAP AI Agent Hub automatically discovers AI agents, LLMs, and MCP servers from the platforms where they are built and deployed. Supported sources include Microsoft Entra ID, Google Agent Engine, Amazon Bedrock, Microsoft Azure API Center, and, new with this release, ServiceNow and SAP AI Core. All discovered assets are organized in a centralized inventory with classification, status, and architecture context. 

Evaluate and verify AI assets 

Once discovered, AI agents and MCP servers enter your inventory in an unverified state. Structured assessments capture key governance attributes for each asset, including AI risk rating, build type, and compliance mappings. Architecture decisions can be attached directly to each fact sheet, giving you a place to document the rationale behind agent adoption, platform choices, and compliance boundaries — and informing whether an asset should be verified or not. Based on this assessment, you can mark any asset as verified, rejected, or unverified, with a verification badge appearing on each fact sheet. Going forward, the badge will be picked up by the Joule Studio runtime to inform which agents are approved to run in production, and through an integration with SAP Integration Suite, it will indicate which MCP servers are verified for use by your developers. 

Outlook 

We are planning to further enhance SAP AI Agent Hub for it to become the central command center of AI assets, surfacing relevant information from diverse sources and ensuring a seamless user journey to access relevant data: 

  • Observability will give you visibility into the runtime behavior, performance, and usage of your AI agents and models in production, with drill-down into individual sessions via SAP Cloud ALM. 
  • Verification status of agents and MCP servers will be visible in Joule Studio and SAP Integration Suite and indicate safe usage.  
  • Identity and access control, built on SAP Cloud Identity Services, will enable you to centrally manage access, identities, and policies for AI agents across your organization. 
  • Agent mining, via an integration with SAP Signavio, will help you understand how agents execute tasks across defined process steps, measuring conformance and coverage. 
  • Org chart and skills mapping via SAP SuccessFactors will allow you to map AI agent deployment to business units and organizational structures, helping you understand workforce impact as agent adoption scales. 

With this, we continue to bring more visibility to your AI ecosystem to give you the confidence to scale your AI strategies.

entry.title: Embedding Custom Service List Views in Application UI
entry.content:
This feature enables administrators to embed custom service list views within both standard and custom UIs via adaptation configuration, with support for passing contextual parameters from the host UI.

Business Benefits:

By embedding relevant custom service lists views directly in the application UI   where users need them, employees spend less time navigating between screens and more time focusing on value-added tasks. Also ensures consistency with the native look and feel of the application.

Passing parameters from the host UI enables users to see contextually filtered or relevant records automatically.

Example Scenario

Consider a work order management system where each work order is linked to an employee. An employee needs quick access to view all work orders assigned to them without navigating away from their current workspace.

Solution: Embed the work order list view directly in the employee's details, automatically filtered by their employee ID to display only their assigned work orders.

Please Note : This blog focuses exclusively on embedding the list view component —not on building the underlying work order example service itself.

How to do:

  • Metadata JSON

The work order-to-employee(project Lead) relationship is defined in the service metadata JSON configuration, establishing the association between work orders and their assigned employees.

{

                    "name": "projectLead",

                    "dataType": "OBJECT",

                    "creatable": true,

                    "updatable": true,

                    "nullable": true,

                    "sortable": false,

                    "filterable": true,

                    "searchable": false,

                    "label": "Project Lead",

                    "objectDefinition": [

                        {

                            "name": "id",

                            "dataType": "STRING",

                            "creatable": true,

                            "updatable": true,

                            "nullable": true,

                            "sortable": false,

                            "filterable": true,

                            "searchable": false,

                            "label": "Project Lead ID",

                            "dataFormat": "UUID",

                            "descriptionAttribute": "formattedName",

                            "keyType": "FOREIGN",

                            "objectReference": {

                                "targetAttribute": "id",

                                "targetEntity": "sap.crm.md.employeeservice.entity.employee",

                                "keyGroup": "projectLead",

                                "targetService": "sap.crm.md.service.employeeService",

                                "sourceEntity": "customer.ssc.workorderservice.entity.workOrder",

                                "associationType": "ASSOCIATION",

                                "sourceAttribute": "projectLead.id"

                            },

                            "analyticsRelevant": true,

                            "labelTextId": "workOrder.projectLead_id",

                            "searchWeightage": 1

                        },

                        {

                            "name": "displayId",

                            "dataType": "STRING",

                            "creatable": false,

                            "updatable": false,

                            "nullable": true,

                            "sortable": true,

                            "filterable": false,

                            "searchable": true,

                            "displayId": true,

                            "label": "Employee Display ID",

                            "dataFormat": "ALPHANUMERIC",

                            "objectReference": {

                                "associationType": "REFERENCE",

                                "targetAttribute": "displayId",

                                "keyGroup": "projectLead",

                                "sourceEntity": "customer.ssc.workorderservice.entity.workOrder"

                            },

                            "analyticsRelevant": true,

                            "labelTextId": "workOrder.projectLead_displayId",

                            "searchWeightage": 1

                        },

                        {

                            "name": "formattedName",

                            "dataType": "STRING",

                            "creatable": false,

                            "updatable": false,

                            "nullable": true,

                            "sortable": true,

                            "filterable": false,

                            "searchable": true,

                            "label": "Employee Name",

                            "objectReference": {

                                "associationType": "REFERENCE",

                                "targetAttribute": "formattedName",

                                "keyGroup": "projectLead",

                                "sourceEntity": "customer.ssc.workorderservice.entity.workOrder"

                            },

                            "analyticsRelevant": true,

                            "labelTextId": "workOrder.projectLead_formattedName",

                            "searchWeightage": 1

                        }

                    ],

                    "analyticsRelevant": true,

                    "labelTextId": "workOrder.projectLead",

                    "searchWeightage": 1

                },

To enable filtering by employee, then as shown above in metadata json for the projectLead(employee) ID field must be configured with the filterable property set to true. This marks the field as a valid filter criterion.

In external service handle the filter logic based on employee ID.

  •  Design App configuration

In the design App, add List Card available under Embedded Component section.

 

Select the filter parameters you want to apply to the embedded view.

A dialog will display all available filterable fields from your service metadata—these are fields marked with filterable: true.

For this example, select the Project Lead ID field (which represents the project lead or assigned employee) as your binding parameter.

Confirm and save your design changes to the application. The embedded component now knows which parameters to filter by.

  • Add Embedded component

Open the desired UI application where you want to embed the work order list view .Add a new section to the UI layout. Click Add Embedded Component.

A list displays all custom services that have embedded component (EC) added in their respective design apps.

Select the work order custom service from this list, then add its list view component to the section.

Click on bind parameters icon and bind the employee ID. This step is similar to mashup parameter binding.

Click on apply. Exit adaptation mode.  The embedded list view is displayed on UI as shown below.

              Employee ID is sent as Filter Parameter in Request :

 

When a filter parameter is passed from the UI to the embedded component, the external service must intercept and process this request(get request with filter parameter). The external service returns the filtered dataset back which is displayed in the embedded component on the UI. Filter parameters are optional and can be configured as per use case.

Note: Since the embedded component feature is available for custom UIs, in this example EC is added in workorder custom UI for demonstration. .If the embedded component feature is not available for your desired standard UI application, please raise a feature request against the specific application component.

Help Document

entry.title: Talent Intelligence Hub Certificates: Your Top Questions Answered
entry.content:
The new Certificates feature in Talent Intelligence Hub, which is part of the 1H 2026 release, enables organizations to manage certifications within the Growth Portfolio experience. Employees can add and maintain certifications relevant to their roles, while administrators can configure certificate types, validity periods, reminders, and workflows. The feature is designed to support ongoing credential management, improve visibility of employee certifications, and provide a foundation for future integrations and reporting enhancements.

General Setup & Configuration

1. How are certificates stored and managed?

Certificates are created as standard attributes in the Attributes Library and then mapped to employees. Administrators must first configure certificate records in the library.

2. Can users add certificates to their Growth Portfolio themselves?

Yes, provided the certificate already exists in the Attributes Library.

Notifications & Expiry Management

1. How is the expiration date derived?

The expiration date is calculated based on the certificate’s issue date and the validity period configured in the Attributes Library.

2. Are expiry reminder settings global or configurable per certificate?

Current preview setup uses global reminder settings. Future enhancements are expected to allow certificate-level overrides.

3. Can multiple reminder notifications be configured (for example 90, 30, and 7 days)?

Notification timing can be configured, and frequency settings such as daily, weekly or monthly are also available.

4. If a certificate expires, is the attached document automatically deleted?

No. Documents remain stored unless manually removed or overwritten.

Skills, Roles & Growth Portfolio

1. Are skills automatically added when a certificate is earned?

 Yes, Skills mapped to the certificate are added (provided it respects the target criteria) to Employee’s growth portfolio when certificate is added .

2. Can certificates be mass uploaded to employees’ Growth Portfolios?

Planned for future enhancement.

3. Is there a tool for bulk updating certificate data?

Planned for future release.

4. Can certificates be mapped to roles?

Planned for 2H2026 release.

5. Can a certificate be made mandatory for a role?

Planned for 2H2026 release.

6. Is it mandatory to associate a skill with a certificate?

No. Associating a skill with a certificate is optional. Certificates can be created and managed independently without requiring a linked skill.

Storage & Attachments

1. Where are uploaded certificate documents stored?

Documents are stored in the Document Management Service.

2. Is there a storage limit for certificate uploads?

A per-file size limit of 2 MB is allowed now. Overall storage follows tenant licensing/storage settings.

Advanced Scenarios

1. Can an administrator remove an outdated lower-level certification when a higher-level one is earned?

Yes, certificates can be removed from an employee’s Growth Portfolio.

2. Is there support for certification equivalency or higher-level progression?

Under evaluation.

3. What happens if a skill linked to a certificate normally requires workflow approval? Will it be added automatically or go through approval?

If the skill is added through a certificate association, it will be added automatically and will not trigger a workflow approval. Currently, workflow approvals are triggered only when a user directly requests a skill rating change through the UI.

Existing Systems & Migration

1. How does this Certificates feature relate to existing certificate sections in Employee Profile, or to curricula in LMS?

The Certificates feature is currently standalone and does not integrate with existing profile background elements or Learning currently. This is a roadmap item.

2. Can certificates from Employee Profile be migrated automatically?

This is being considered for future enhancement.

3. Can certificates already stored in employee documents be imported automatically?

Currently, the Certificates feature operates independently and does not integrate with Employee Profile background elements or Learning.

Integrations

1. Is integration with Learning available?

Considered for future roadmap.

2. Can third-party vendors automatically fetch certificates?

Not currently supported.

3. Can certificates from TIH be stored in external document management systems?

Currently out of scope

Reporting & Analytics

1.Is reporting available for certificates?

The Talent Intelligence Hub schema available for Story Reports now includes the new Certificate attribute. For additional details please refer to Available Data (Schema) in Story Report | SAP Help

 2. Is integration with Learning Analytics dashboards planned?

No current plans have been confirmed.

Access & User Scope

1. Are certificates available to external users or onboardees?

Not currently supported.

2. Are certificate workflows available without an Employee Central license?

Yes. Certificate workflows are based on MDF workflows, which are part of the foundational platform services and do not depend on an Employee Central license.

Key resources

Certificates | What’s New Viewer

Certificates | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Excel Upload Functionality for Payment Entries in SAP
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

Currently, payment entries are being created manually in SAP, which is time-consuming and increases the risk of manual errors.

Requirement:
We require an Excel upload template/functionality to upload bulk payment entries into SAP.

Expected Functionality:

  • Upload payment data through an Excel file/template.
  • Validation of mandatory fields before upload.
  • Error log/report for failed records.
  • Successful records to be posted automatically or moved for approval.
  • Standardized upload format/template to be shared with users.
  • Proper authorization control for upload access.

Business Justification:

This enhancement will reduce manual effort, improve processing efficiency, and minimize data entry errors during bulk payment processing.
Please review and confirm the possible solution along with the required configuration or development effort.

Regards,
Nandini

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content: Very interesting @MatthiasL . No HANA DB patch updates?? Wow!! It is weird to even call SAP EC Payroll system as cloud. It doesnt fit any definition of the cloud other than being in a network and being charged a subscription fee. It is just an ECC system sitting in a SAP data center (and that too not in the same data center as your SF data centers but separate). Also, the EC Payroll code base has not been optimized to leverage the capability of HANA DB; no actual benefit out of using HANA DB which is probably the reason they haven't bothered to migrate to HANA DB.

entry.title: SQL Anywhere on Linux
entry.content:
Hi, ALL,

I have installed SQL Anywhere 17 full on my Windows box.

 

Now yesterday I downloaded the SQL Anywhere client on one of my Linux boxes and successfully installed it.

I already have unixODBC set up on that box and so I tried to set up a connection and then tried to run my program.

 

I used 64 bit driver as my program is compiled as 64 bit.

 

Unfortunately the program crashed inside SQLAllocHandle() call of the driver.

 

I will not have access to this machine until Friday, but does this looks familiar to anybody?

How do I set up the unixODBC with SQLAnywhere to get a successful connection?

 

I read the docs and gather that the server comes with its own Driver Manager implementation, but I'm using only the client.

 

Thank you.

 

P.S.: This is the code I'm using:

 

[code]

int ODBCDatabase::Connect(const std::wstring &selectedDSN, std::vector<std::wstring> &dbList, std::vector<std::wstring> &errorMsg)
{
int result = 0, bufferSize = 1024;
std::vector<SQLWCHAR *> errorMessage;
SQLWCHAR connectStrIn[sizeof(SQLWCHAR) * 255], driver[1024], dsn[1024], dbType[1024];
SQLSMALLINT OutConnStrLen;
SQLRETURN ret;
SQLUSMALLINT options;
SQLWCHAR *user = nullptr, *password = nullptr;
SQLWCHAR driverName[1024];
SQLWCHAR dbName[1024];
SQLWCHAR userName[1024];
std::wstring connectingDSN, connectingUser = L"", connectingPassword = L"";
pimpl.m_type = L"ODBC";
pimpl.m_pgLogFile = L"";
if( !odbc_pimpl )
odbc_pimpl = new ODBCImpl;
std::wstring::size_type pos = selectedDSN.find( L';' );
if( pos == std::wstring::npos )
connectingDSN = selectedDSN;
else
{
std::wstring temp = selectedDSN.substr( selectedDSN.find( L"DSN=" ) + 4 );
connectingDSN = temp.substr( 0, temp.find( L";") );
// connectingDSN = selectedDSN.substr( 0, pos );
temp = temp.substr( temp.find( L"UID=" ) + 4 );
connectingUser = temp.substr( 0, temp.find( L";" ) );
temp = temp.substr( temp.find( L"PWD=" ) + 4 );
connectingPassword = temp.substr( 0, temp.find( L";" ) );
user = new SQLWCHAR[connectingUser.length() + 2];
password = new SQLWCHAR[connectingPassword.length() + 2];
memset( user, '\0', connectingUser.length() + 2 );
memset( password, '\0', connectingPassword.length() + 2 );
uc_to_str_cpy( user, connectingUser );
uc_to_str_cpy( password, connectingPassword );
}
m_connectString = new SQLWCHAR[sizeof(SQLWCHAR) * 1024];
memset( dsn, 0, sizeof( dsn ) );
memset( connectStrIn, 0, sizeof( connectStrIn ) );
memset( driver, 0, sizeof( driver ) );
uc_to_str_cpy( dsn, connectingDSN.c_str() );
ret = SQLAllocHandle( SQL_HANDLE_ENV, SQL_NULL_HENV, &m_env );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
{
errorMsg.push_back( L"Failed to allocate memory for the connection" );
return 1;
}
ret = SQLSetEnvAttr( m_env, SQL_ATTR_ODBC_VERSION, (SQLPOINTER) SQL_OV_ODBC3, SQL_IS_INTEGER );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, ENV_ERROR );
result = 1;
}
else
{
ret = SQLAllocHandle( SQL_HANDLE_DBC, m_env, &m_hdbc );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, ENV_ERROR );
return 1;
}
else
{
if( !GetDriverForDSN( dsn, driver, errorMsg ) )
{
std::unique_ptr<SQLWCHAR[]> tempPostgres( new SQLWCHAR[13] );
memset( tempPostgres.get(), '\0', 13 );
uc_to_str_cpy( tempPostgres.get(), L"PostgreSQL " );
if( connectStrIn[0] == '\0' )
{
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, L"DSN=" );
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, connectingDSN.c_str() );
}
if( equal( tempPostgres.get(), driver ) )
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, L";UseServerSidePrepare=1;ShowSystemTables=1;" );
std::wstring tempmySQL;
str_to_uc_cpy( tempmySQL, driver );
if( tempmySQL.find( L"myodbc" ) != std::wstring::npos )
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, L";NO_SCHEMA=0;NO_CATALOG=0" );
if( user && password )
{
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, L";UID=" );
copy_uc_to_uc( connectStrIn, user );
uc_to_str_cpy( connectStrIn, L";PWD=" );
copy_uc_to_uc( connectStrIn, password );
}
delete[] user;
user = nullptr;
delete[] password;
password = nullptr;
ret = SQLSetConnectAttr( m_hdbc, SQL_LOGIN_TIMEOUT, (SQLPOINTER)50, 0 );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO && ret != SQL_NO_DATA )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, 2 );
result = 1;
}
if( !result )
{
#ifdef _WIN32
options = m_ask ? SQL_DRIVER_COMPLETE : SQL_DRIVER_NOPROMPT;
#else
options = SQL_DRIVER_NOPROMPT;
#endif
ret = SQLDriverConnect( m_hdbc, m_handle, connectStrIn, SQL_NTS, m_connectString, 1024, &OutConnStrLen, options );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO && ret != SQL_NO_DATA )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, CONN_ERROR );
result = 1;
}
if( ret == SQL_NO_DATA )
{
errorMsg.push_back( L"Connection cancelled" );
return 0;
}
}

[/code]

entry.title: How to Understand SAP Green Ledger in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Why SAP Green Ledger Matters

Sustainability and financial data are often managed separately and making it difficult to align environmental impact with business performance.

SAP Green Ledger bridges this gap by integrating carbon data directly into financial accounting processes within SAP S/4HANA. This allows organizations to combine financial and environmental insights in one unified view.

With SAP Green Ledger, businesses can:

  • Align carbon emissions with financial data
  • Increase transparency for reporting and decision-making
  • Support carbon accounting alongside financial accounting
  • Enable better planning and analysis of sustainability performance

What You’ll Learn in This Course

This short, 45-minute course introduces the fundamentals of SAP Green Ledger:

  1. Financial Integration
    Understand how SAP Green Ledger relates to and integrates with financial accounting in SAP S/4HANA.
  2. Key Features and Capabilities
    Explore core functionalities, including integration, analytics, and planning capabilities.
  3. Business Value
    Learn how combining financial and sustainability data supports more informed and strategic decision-making.

Who Should Take the Course?

This course is ideal for:

  • Business Users working with finance and sustainability data
  • Consultants supporting SAP S/4HANA and sustainability solutions

No prerequisites are required, making it a great starting point for beginners.

Conclusion

Bridge the gap between sustainability and finance and explore the course “Outlining Green Ledger in SAP S/4HANA” here.

entry.title: Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management - What's New in 2605
entry.content:
Greetings SAP Supply Chain Management community!

Release 2605 delivers meaningful innovations across the SAP Supply Chain Management portfolio—helping organizations improve visibility, increase efficiency, and respond faster to change. In this post, we highlight what’s new across key products and, more importantly, point you to the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management, where you can explore detailed feature descriptions, walk-thru's, and enablement content to support your adoption journey.

Table of Contents

  • What’s New in SAP Supply Chain Management
  • SAP Asset Performance Management
  • SAP Business Network
  • SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP Field Service Management
  • SAP Integrated Business Planning
  • SAP Integrated Product Development
  • SAP Logistics Management
  • What’s New in the Release Navigator
  • Guided Walk-Thru Tutorials for SAP Integrated Business Planning
  • Connect with Us

 

What’s New in SAP Supply Chain Management

SAP Asset Performance Management

SAP Asset Performance Management strengthens Asset Health and Indicator Monitoring by improving transparency and troubleshooting. Enhancements make it easier to understand rule execution, data set usage, and indicator behavior, while tighter integration with maintenance notifications and orders helps connect asset health trends to real operational actions.

🔗Review feature details, walkthroughs, and guidance in the Release Navigator for SAP Asset Performance Management.

 

SAP Business Network

Updates in SAP Business Network focus on usability, scalability, and accuracy. Confirmation workflow filtering is more flexible, reporting capacity has increased significantly, and service procurement and integration scenarios are better supported—ensuring smoother collaboration between buyers and suppliers.

🔗Explore the latest feature documentation and adoption resources in the Release Navigator for SAP Business Network.

 

SAP Digital Manufacturing

SAP Digital Manufacturing delivers a major expansion of POD 2.0, enabling greater flexibility and control on the shop floor. Operators benefit from enhanced execution, reporting, and compliance capabilities, while manufacturers can leverage AI-assisted engineering, improved rule configuration, and stronger automation across production processes.

🔗Dive deeper into these innovations in the Release Navigator for SAP Digital Manufacturing, including guided resources for POD 2.0 and EBR scenarios.

 

SAP Field Service Management

Planning, dispatching, and intelligence capabilities in SAP Field Service Management continue to evolve with richer map-based tools, optimized assignment handling, and new AI-powered support via Joule. The release also expands configuration, extensibility, and language support—along with the solution’s relaunch.

🔗Learn how to enable and apply these features in the Release Navigator for SAP Field Service Management.

 

SAP Integrated Business Planning

SAP Integrated Business Planning introduces enhancements across administration, integration, planning, and analytics. Improved system operations, stronger SAP Datasphere integration, expanded real-time capabilities, and new planning and scenario features help organizations plan with greater precision and confidence.

🔗Access curated feature explanations, activation steps, and enablement assets in the Release Navigator for SAP Integrated Business Planning.

 

SAP Integrated Product Development

SAP Integrated Product Development enhances collaboration, formulation, and specification management, with added AI-powered search, improved workflow governance, and a more streamlined user experience. These updates help teams work more efficiently across complex, cross-functional product development processes.

🔗Explore full feature details and guidance in the Release Navigator for SAP Integrated Product Development.

 

SAP Logistics Management

Enhancements in SAP Logistics Management improve integration, transparency, and execution across outbound, warehouse, and configuration processes. New navigation, scheduling, and data consistency improvements help streamline daily logistics operations.

🔗Find detailed documentation and activation guidance in the Release Navigator for SAP Logistics Management.

 

What’s New in the Release Navigator

Guided Walk-Thru Tutorials for SAP Integrated Business Planning

The new SAP Integrated Business Planning walk-thru's focused on upgrade tools are designed to help customers navigate upgrades with clarity and confidence. These guided experiences focus on how to assess upgrade impacts, validate key data and integration changes, and apply recommended checks before and after moving to a new release, reducing risk while keeping planning processes running smoothly. All upgrade-focused walk-thru's can be found on the Get Assistance page within the Release Navigator, where they are organized alongside supporting documentation and best‑practice guidance.

👉 Visit the Release Navigator, open the Get Assistance page, and explore the new upgrade walk-thru's to prepare your landscape for a successful upgrade.

 

Connect with Us

Your feedback helps shape future Release Navigator content. We encourage you to:

  • Complete our feedback survey
  • Share ideas or questions in the comments
  • Learn more about the Readiness@Scale team
  • Explore additional SAP enablement content

Thank you for your continued engagement.

Best Run,
Readiness@Scale
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Multi-Stage Advanced intercompany process with External Suppliers
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We would appreciate expert input from the SAP community on a design challenge we are currently evaluating in our S/4HANA landscape.
We are evaluating SAP S/4HANA Multi-Level Advanced Intercompany (MLAIC) to support a multi-stage intercompany (buy-sell) chain in a turntable setup (multiple company codes with valuated SIT and end-to-end document flow).

While MLAIC works well for internal intercompany scenarios, we are facing design challenges when integrating an external supplier (e.g., CMO) into the process chain.

⚠️ Key Design Gaps / Challenges

  • No standard integration pattern for external supplier involvement
    • Missing linkage between MLAIC document chain and external procurement processes
  • Break in end-to-end document flow orchestration
    • External supplier operates outside MLAIC → requires additional trigger logic (e.g., via MRP / demand planning) to continue the process chain
  • Goods movement & ownership handling
    • Unclear orchestration of GI/GR postings and ownership transfer (valuated SIT vs. externally triggered shipment events). In other words, how can we do an automatic Goods receipt and Goods Issue at Intermediate company when the delivering company has physically dispatched the goods to the receiving company.
  • Tax / Localization complexity
    • Handling multi-VAT registrations of a central entity in cross-border flows
    • Dynamic determination of tax setup in external supplier → intercompany chain scenarios
  • Planning ↔ Execution integration
    • Dependency on MRP and planning triggers to connect external procurement with MLAIC execution
    • No standard pattern across planning → logistics → FI integration

❓ Questions to the Community

  • Has anyone implemented MLAIC with external supplier scenarios?
  • What design approaches are used to connect external procurement flows with MLAIC orchestration?
  • How are valuated SIT, GI/GR timing, and ownership transfer handled in such hybrid setups?
  • Any best practices for managing complex VAT/tax determination in these flows?

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:

That said, we got our system nearly 2 years ago and I think in November there was a security note for HANA DB and we were like, shouldn't we upgrade this? This wasn't an option. After a long, long discussion after pointing out they promise to at least upgrade it once a year (it had been 1,5 year at this point) they finally upgraded it - explaining they wanted to test is properly. But it leaves a bit of a sour taste. You as a customer are supposed to take care of security notes that are not criticial (lower than CVSS score 9), but if you request it - they won't oblige.

Similarly the SAP router we bought with it, is on a version of 2024. We tried to request an update and where told plain and simple that this was not an option..

Guess it's still better than 7 year old stuff (I realised it was 6 years old when we were facing a decision so must be older now).

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Transport Management for Company Cod...
entry.content:
Dear @ASMAE3,

In Public Cloud there is no standard mechanism to deactivate, delete, or selectively exclude a confirmed Company Code from being transported across the landscape. Both the organizational-structure model in CBC and the transport handling in the 3-system landscape are designed in a way that makes this kind of per-Company-Code include/exclude approach not feasible. The recommended path is to keep the incorrect Company Codes in place, retire them from operational use and add the new, correct legal entities alongside them

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/c38e987da8cf471f8888d703938f2ef1/SHIP3.0/en-US/SAPCentralBusinessConfiguration_ProjectExperience_en-US.pdf

It is worth distinguishing the project status:

  1. If the incorrect Company Code has not yet been confirmed in CBC this is the right moment to correct the organizational structure before deployment. The correction should be done in CBC before the organizational structure is confirmed and deployed to the target system
  2. If the incorrect Company Code has already been confirmed in CBC, it cannot be deleted or unassigned through the standard Set Up Organizational Structure process. Even if it has not yet reached all downstream systems this is no longer a normal delete and exclude from transport scenario
  3. If the organizational structure has already been deployed to the customizing tenant / downstream landscape the Company Code is part of the configuration baseline. A later transport with new Company Codes will not remove the old incorrect Company Codes. Public Cloud transport handling is transport-request based, not a business-level mechanism to include one Company Code and exclude another one. CBC transport requests also have to follow dependency and sequence rules so unwanted CBC content is not handled by simply skipping selected Company Codes

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3547695

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3482742

The recommended approach, assuming the incorrect Company Codes are already confirmed or deployed:

  • Create the corrected legal entities with new Company Code IDs in CBC, validate them, and move them through the standard CBC transport path. Adding new organizational units after the initial setup is a fully supported CBC scenario

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003126190

  • Mark the incorrect Company Codes with the description DO NOT USE, block G/L account postings and restrict their usage in master data

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3138435

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3580500

  • Restrict access at the business-role level Company Code is supported as a restriction field in business roles so end users assigned to operational roles can be prevented from selecting the incorrect Company Codes

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2513783

  • If the project requirement is that the incorrect Company Codes must not exist at all in the QAS/PROD landscape this needs to be raised with the SAP customer-facing representative before further deployment because it cannot be solved by standard configuration or by selective transport

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Future-Dated Records on Terminated Employees: 1H 2026 Finally Closes a Long-Standing Gap.

Hi @shaktibarath, this is a great enhancement, but we cannot get it to work.

Don't we need to set this new field to Yes in the Event Reasons we want to have removed after a Termination?

entry.title: 🚀 Exciting News: Updated E-learning courses on SAP Sales Cloud V2!
entry.content:
The world of sales is transforming faster than ever, powered by AI, intelligent automation, and the relentless pursuit of sales excellence. To help you stay ahead of the curve, we are thrilled to announce the updated release of our self-paced learning courses for SAP Sales Cloud Version 2, now available for free on learning.sap.com!

These are not just minor touch-ups — these updates bring fresh, relevant, and enriched content designed to meet you exactly where you are in your SAP Sales Cloud journey.

From system setup to advanced configuration, this is your go-to resource for mastering the platform from the inside out.

entry.title: Re: Use of Pegging for Demand-to-Procurement Traceability...
entry.content:
Good day @MarkusDaum 

Thank you for your question.

For your exact requirement, there is an important distinction to take note of:

If you procure to unrestricted stock because of a supplier minimum order quantity, the purchase order is normally a stock receipt, not a demand-specific receipt. In that standard make-to-stock logic, the system does not create a fixed and audit-stable one-to-one relationship saying:

PO 123 covers Reservation A, PO 127 covers Reservation B, and PO 129 covers Reservation C.

The stock is planned and consumed at material/Plant/MRP area level. Therefore, the standard stock list or material coverage view can show the sequence of requirements and receipts, but it does not provide stable business traceability between a stock PO and one specific reservation.

That been said, it can be said that, the Public Cloud does not provide a stable one-to-one assignment showing exactly which purchase order covers which individual reservation for standard stock procurement scenario. If the purchase order is created to unrestricted stock because of a supplier minimum order quantity, the receipt is treated as stock at material/plant or MRP area level. The system can show receipts and requirements in sequence, but it does not create a permanent business link such as PO 123 equals Reservation A.

Pegging can provide better planning visibility, especially in advanced planning scenarios. However, dynamic pegging can be recalculated during planning runs, and you should not treat it as permanent traceability. Fixed pegging is more stable, but it should be used only where the planning design requires it and where the capability is available in the activated scope.

What I recommend is as follows:

  1. Make use of Monitor Material Coverage or Manage material Coverage to review receipt and demand sequence.        
  2. Confirm whether the PO is stock procurement or demand-specific procurement.
  3. If you require stable traceability, evaluate a demand-specific process such as account-assigned procurement, make-to-order stock, project stock, or another supported requirement-specific process.
  4. If you require advanced planning, first confirm your license and scope activation with your implementation partner or SAP.
  5. In Manage Product Mast Data, check the Advanced Planning section and resolve the system message about creating the plant as an SCM location.                                                                                                                                  
  6. Do not rely on dynamic pegging as an audit-stable PO-to-reservation assignment.
  7. You may consider raising an SAP Customer Influence if your requirement is mandatory and none of the standard processes fits your demand. Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to redefine RDS based OData services?
entry.content:
@Abh_2 the method I have described in my blog post has been tried out for RDS based services that have to be extended via additional entities that itself are being added via RDS.

What I assume is that you just had a look at the code that my report generates and picked up the idea of providing an updated XML that contains an additional entry for an association.

This I have never tried and I don't have the time to look deeper into this topic.

However when using RDS additonal methods are being generated, especially the handling of the get_last_modfied( ) method which is called in case when RDS is used in method DEFINE_RDS_4().

So you would have in addition to redefine get_last_modified() to make sure that the changed metadata is being loaded.

Regeneration of a SEGW project that has been delivered by SAP is not a good idea since this is technially a modifcation since it is tried to regenerate classes in the SAP namespace. You should thus refrain from doing so.

  METHOD get_last_modified.
    " 1st step: call SADL with the extended XML
    TRY.
        DATA(lv_sadl_modified) = CAST cl_sadl_gw_model_exposure( if_sadl_gw_model_exposure_data~get_model_exposure( ) )->get_last_modified( ).
      CATCH cx_sadl_exposure_error.
        ASSERT 0 = 1.
    ENDTRY.

    " 2nd step: call default and integrate
    rv_last_modified = super->get_last_modified( ).
    IF rv_last_modified < lv_sadl_modified.
      rv_last_modified = lv_sadl_modified.
    ENDIF.
ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: How to create an Aging Analysis report using a User selected Key Date leveraging SAC Date difference
entry.content:
Problem definition:

In the area of reporting, for example in aging Reports there is always a requirement to calculate days between 2 dates like posting date and key date or order date and delivery date etc. In SAP Analytics Cloud generally this leads to a story defined calculation of type Date Difference. This calculated Measure definition supports the following options.

  • Granularity - This is the granularity of the date difference calculation. Supported values are Year, Month, Date, Hour, seconds, minutes.
  • Time A and Time B -  These are the 2 dates of which we want to calculate the difference between. They support Current Date, Current Timestamp and Date type dimensions of the model.
  • Dimension Context - This is the dimension context under which the difference is calculated.
  • Result Aggregation - How the difference should be aggregated. You can keep it to AVERAGE or change as per requirement.

This calculation works perfectly if you want to calculate difference between 2 existing date type dimensions, or one date type dimensions and current date/time. The calculation does not support any Input control for dimensions and hence there is no option to calculate date difference between a date dimension and a user selected key date.

Technical Solution:

We will try to build a workaround solution to calculate date difference between user selected date and Date dimension which can help us create aging report with key date as selection.

In SAC stories calculation editor, date difference calculation is done by defining a calculated measure of type Date difference and below is how the setup looks like.

The only supported option for 2 dates are Current Date, Current Timestamp and Date dimension of the model. No option for any input control selection.

We want to calculate date difference between Date dimension and user selected date.

  • Define User defined key Date selection

To capture the user selection for key date we will define a Date Input control of type range and will use custom calendar. This will give us a calendar picker to select date. 

The resultant date input control can be hidden from outline view and also disconnected from any widgets by keeping the below setting for  the Input control Linked Analysis. Its just a place holder input control with no filtering affect on any Tables or widgets.

The above will help me get the below for key date selection. ( still waiting for SAC to provide calendar pickers to select dates ( From/ To and date ranges, ....topic for another day)

Now we have created a mechanism to allow users to select key date. Our next task is to pass this key date selection to our calculation.

The date difference calculation only supports dimensions of type date. Lets build up a date dimension with our user selected key date. 

SAC supports custom dimensions. The issue is custom dimensions also do not support input controls or variables. Lets workaround this problem too.

  • Define a Script variable to be used in calculated measures

Define a script variable of type integer as shown below. This variable will store the integer pattern for selected date. If user selects 15 March 2024, it should store 15042024. We will define the logic to extract this in subsequent steps.

  • Define a Text Box and assign the dynamic text binding to the selected Key date

This will help us to read the selected key date and extract the integer pattern from it. 

Output: The user selection is assigned as shown below.

  • Define a logic to extract integer date pattern from user selected date (Mar 15, 2024)

 we will use some java scripting to derive the formatted date from above. We can assign the below code to a button which will help us calculate the value and also refresh the application.

// 1. Read selected Key Date

var seldate=Text_1.getPlainText(); // Text Box value with dynamic text binding

var monthName = seldate.substr(0, 3);
var day = seldate.substr(4, 2);
var year = seldate.substr(8, 4);

// 2. Map month name to number
var monthNum = "";
if (monthName === "Jan") { monthNum = "01"; }
else if (monthName === "Feb") { monthNum = "02"; }
else if (monthName === "Mar") { monthNum = "03"; }
else if (monthName === "Apr") { monthNum = "04"; }
else if (monthName === "May") { monthNum = "05"; }
else if (monthName === "Jun") { monthNum = "06"; }
else if (monthName === "Jul") { monthNum = "07"; }
else if (monthName === "Aug") { monthNum = "08"; }
else if (monthName === "Sep") { monthNum = "09"; }
else if (monthName === "Oct") { monthNum = "10"; }
else if (monthName === "Nov") { monthNum = "11"; }
else if (monthName === "Dec") { monthNum = "12"; }

// 3. Reorder to DDMMYYYY
var formattedDate = day + monthNum + year;

console.log(seldate);
console.log(formattedDate);

SV_KeyDate=ConvertUtils.stringToInteger(formattedDate); // Script variable assignment

console.log(SV_KeyDate);

Application.refreshData(); //Trigger Refresh after assigning value to variable

 

  • Create a calculated Measure using the Script variable define earlier (SV_KeyDate).

Use @ to read the script variable

  •  Create a calculated Dimension of type Measure based dimension using the above calculated Measure

Configure to use measure values as dimension members. Keep decimal as 0.

  • Define another calculated dimension using the previous calculated dimension.

TODATE(SUBSTRING([d/"MBD_KD"].[p/ID],0,2)+"-"+SUBSTRING([d/"MBD_KD"].[p/ID],2,2)+"-"+SUBSTRING([d/"MBD_KD"].[p/ID],4,4),"dd-mm-yyyy")

So, our scripting will assign 15032024 to script variable SV_KeyDate. Calculated Measure will be assigned this value. Next our first calculated dimension will get "15032024" as member id. Next we use TODATE() ( Syntax: ToDate("date_string";"input_format") ) function to create a date member "15-03-2024" using "15032024". The above formula creates the date member in correct format.

  • Define the Final Calculated Measure for Date difference

 Now the calculation editor allows us to select the previous defined calculated dimension as one of the date selections in Date difference calculation.

 

  • Final Result

Excel calculation of Days Difference

 key date
Date15/03/202410/05/2024
01/01/202474130
04/01/202471127
06/01/202469125
07/01/202468124
10/01/202465121
03/02/20244197
05/02/20243995
08/02/20243692
11/02/20243389

Limitations: 

Currently The story does not refresh automatically on change in Key Date selections. Button interaction has been leveraged to calculate the dimensions and the measures used in the calculation and also trigger the table refresh. This part could be improved with a scripting workaround.

Summary:

This way we can achieve calculating date difference calculation between a standard date dimension value and user defined date selection. 
If you think this post has helped you, please like and share your thoughts in the comments section below. Incase of any queries please ask the same in the comments section and I would be happy to answer the same.

Thanks for reading. I hope you enjoyed the blog.

Check the SAP Analytics Cloud topic page for more up to date information https://community.sap.com/topics/cloud-analytics

follow and read other informative blog posts on SAC browse the below link

https://blogs.sap.com/tags/67838200100800006884/

 

Regards

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation: Cannot add rules in Dec...

The plus sign is just an image. Use the "Create Rule" button which sits below the image. Scroll down in the window if you don't see it. After you create and save at least one rule, you will see the "Create Rule" button/link on the top right of the Rules tab.

Additional tips: It looks like you are configuring an SAP Fieldglass approval decision. If so, (1) ensure your output Name is "Response" and (2) the list checkbox is selected to the right of the output parameter.  You can reference the documentation and videos via the links below.

  • BTP Business Rules Service for SAP Fieldglass (PDF)
  • Integrating SAP Fieldglass and BTP SBPA for Enhanced Approval Workflows (VIDEO)
  • Integrating SAP Fieldglass and BTP SBPA for Workflow On-Page Validations (VIDEO)

Below is an image of the desired format for the output parameter, with "Response" in the Name field and the List checkbox selected.

entry.title: Re: Webi: Unable to sort Cross Table break sections using...
entry.content:
Removing the default break sort may solve your issue.  But if not, here is a similar example you could follow:

I do not know what to call your 2nd column, but in my example I am using eFashion data and let's use "Category" as an example for that column. 

 

Add a new column to the left of your total column in the crosstable and add a variable that returns the sum or count of Category for each KMA Market

With eFashion data, this is what it would correspond to:

Category Cnt by Line   =  Sum(Count([Category]) In ([Lines])) 

In your case, it may be the Sum(Sum … rather than Sum(count    e.g. =Sum(Sum([CategoryTotal]) In ([KMA Market]))

Confirm that calculation context is working and that the KMA Market subtotal is matching the variable for each row. 

On the break properties of KMA Market, remove the "Apply sort":

This sort would override your preferred sort.

Now select the whole crosstable and add a  Descending sort on the variable.

You can now hide this column, as it is only needed for sorting purposes.

Results:
 

Let us know if this works for you,
~Roxanne

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and ECTR
entry.content:
Thanks for raising this topic, @christian_kokholm5a5d46  — it’s a very real dilemma.
 
You’re absolutely right: SAP Engineering Control Center (ECTR) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition are both positioned as strategic, yet today they do not align seamlessly for R&D‑heavy, CAD‑centric customers.

How SAP positions this today (fact‑based view)

  • SAP ECTR remains an on‑premise solution and is not supported with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition follows a strict SaaS / clean‑core model with limited support for tightly coupled, on‑premise engineering integrations.
  • As a result, there is no like‑for‑like ECTR replacement in the public cloud today.
This means customers with deep mechanical/electrical CAD integration requirements must make architectural trade‑offs, not just technical ones.

What we see as realistic paths today

Based on partner project experience, customers typically land in one of these patterns:

1) Stay on‑premise or move to private cloud (to preserve ECTR)

  • Chosen by engineering‑driven manufacturers where CAD‑PLM‑ERP integration is mission‑critical.
  • Provides continuity of ECTR‑based processes.
  • Comes at the cost of slower SaaS innovation cadence and higher lifecycle management effort.

2) Hybrid  or two-tier landscape (Public Cloud ERP + on‑premise ERP with ECTR)

  • Often used as a transition architecture, not a pure target state.
  • ERP core moves to public cloud, while engineering stays on‑premise.
  • Introduces integration complexity, possibly dual master data governance, and operational overhead.
  • Viable, but needs very clear ownership and long‑term intent.

3) Rethink engineering integration using cloud‑native services

  • Some customers explore public‑cloud PLM or document‑centric approaches with "looser" CAD integration.
  • Requires process redesign and acceptance of functional differences compared to ECTR.
  • This is not a technical migration, but a business transformation decision.
None of these options is trivial — which is exactly the challenge you describe.

How SAP envision this (reading between the lines, cautiously)

Without speculating on roadmap commitments, what is clear from SAP Cloud ERP principles and implementation project experiences is:
  • Public cloud is optimized for standardized, loosely coupled integrations, not deep CAD control.
  • Hybrid or two-tier scenarios are explicitly supported, but typically positioned as transitional or selective, not always as the end‑state for core engineering.
  • Customers with very advanced R&D requirements are often better served (today) by private cloud or hybrid / two-tier strategies, at least until cloud‑native engineering capabilities mature further.

Practical guidance

For partners advising R&D‑heavy customers, a few lessons learned stand out:
  • Be explicit early about what is not available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition — especially around ECTR‑style integration.
  • Treat this as a Fit‑to‑Standard and scope decision in the Explore Phase of SAP Activate, not a late technical surprise (a recurring cloud project pitfall) [Success Guide for SAP Business Suite | Digital Asset Management]
  • Frame the decision as “process strategy first, deployment model second”, not the other way around.
  • If public cloud is the strategic target, plan for process simplification or decoupling, not technical equivalence.

Open and as yet unanswered

  • There is no published public‑cloud roadmap that confirms ECTR‑equivalent capabilities.
  • Customers who need ECTR‑equivalent capabilities should actively use Customer Influence to make the demand visible.
  • Until then, private cloud and hybrid / two-tier architectures remain valid and necessary for many engineering‑centric customers.
Summary
ECTR and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition are both strategic — but not for the same engineering depth today. For R&D‑heavy customers, the choice is less about technology and more about which compromises are acceptable right now.
AI transparency note
This response was prepared with AI‑assisted drafting and validated against available SAP project experience material. Please always verify against current SAP documentation and roadmap publications before final implementation consideration.
 
If this answer helped, please mark it accordingly.

entry.title: Re: Application- Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document
entry.content:
Thanks for the question, Saloni.
 
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document Fiori app (App ID F0843) is documented primarily for posting goods receipt (GR) by purchasing document and (per the help) the key required location input is the storage location. The step-by-step procedure in SAP Help Portal for “Posting Goods Receipt for the Purchasing Document” explicitly mentions “Specify the storage location” but does not mention a manual storage bin (BIN) input field. [help.sap.com]

What you can try in the app (mobile-friendly)

Check View Settings / personalization
SAP documents that the Post Goods Receipt apps (including Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document – F0843) support View Settings to show/hide available columns and fields on the main page. So, on your mobile device, open the app and look for View Settings (or the initial “set View Settings” dialog when opening the app the first time) and see where the  “storage bin” column is available to add. [help.sap.com]

 

Why this happens (process perspective)

SAP’s Warehouse Management capability in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is designed to manage warehouses “down to storage bin level.”
So if your requirement is to choose between GR-AREA-1 and GR-AREA-2 bins at posting time, you typically need to ensure you are using the process/app that supports bin-level warehouse execution. [help.sap.com]
Sources (SAP Help Portal)
  • Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (App ID F0843) [help.sap.com]
  • Posting Goods Receipt for the Purchasing Document (procedure mentions storage location) [help.sap.com]
  • How to Personalize the View Settings in Post Goods Receipt Apps (includes F0843) [help.sap.com]
  • Warehouse Management overview (bin-level mapping) [help.sap.com]
  • App Extensibility: Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document [help.sap.com]
AI transparency note
This response was prepared with AI‑powered assistance and validated against SAP Help Portal documentation visible at the time of drafting. Please verify against your landscape's release and configuration.
 
If this response was helpful to you, please mark it accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Can we please use the 'old' Custom Analytical Queries...
entry.content:
Thanks for raising this — this is a topic some projects run into.

Short answer

No, there is currently no supported way to re‑enable the “old” Custom Analytical Queries app for creating new queries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. SAP has intentionally consolidated query creation into “Custom Analytical Queries and Services”, and the older app is kept in a read‑only / compatibility mode for existing content.

What this means in practice

  • Custom Analytical Queries (old app)
    • Can still be used to display and consume existing queries
    • Creation of new queries is intentionally disabled
    • Not planned to be re‑opened for creation (design decision, not a bug)
  • Custom Analytical Queries and Services (new app)
    • Is the strategic app going forward
    • Supports query creation and OData exposure in one place
    • Some features from the old app (for example, certain aggregation options on individual elements) are not yet fully on par
This behavior is consistent with SAP’s general approach in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: once a successor app is delivered, creation is moved there and the predecessor is not re‑activated.

Recommended approach

  1. Model with CDS / released views first
    In some cases, missing aggregation behavior can be addressed by:
    • Choosing a different released analytical CDS view as a base, or
    • Adjusting key figure semantics (if available) rather than relying on query‑level aggregation.
  2. Check if the missing aggregation is already planned
    SAP is incrementally closing functional gaps in Custom Analytical Queries and Services.
    Always cross‑check the roadmap on roadmaps.sap.com for Analytics and Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  3. Raise an Influence request (strongly recommended)
    If the missing aggregation options are critical for partner/customer scenarios:
    • Submit a request via Customer Influence
    • Clearly describe:
      • Which aggregation option existed in the old app
      • Why it cannot be modeled with released CDS views today
      • The business impact (e.g. Finance, Inventory, Management Reporting)
    This is the only supported channel to influence parity between the old and new apps.

Bottom line

Re‑enabling the old app for query creation is not supported and not expected.
The only sustainable path is:
  • Use Custom Analytical Queries and Services, and
  • Actively influence SAP to close the remaining functional gaps.
AI transparency note
This response was prepared with AI‑powered assistance and reflects current SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition behavior as observed in implementation projects. Always validate against your landscape’s exact release.
 
If this helps, please mark the answer accordingly. Thanks!

entry.title: How to add validation for a field in PR item in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud (Step by Step)
entry.content:
Introduction:

https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/how-to-add-custom-field-in-purchase-requisition-item-in-sap-s-4hana-public/ba-p/14373930

In our previous blog, we learned about how we can add a field using the custom field app in SAP Public Cloud. In there, we have added a field for the department. Now, the client has requested us to make this field mandatory because the user may have forgotten about it, even though there is a field for department, so if we make it mandatory, then it won't let the user save the PR unless they fill in the department field. We will use the custom logic app.

In this blog, we will cover :

> How to add validation for a field in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud using the CUSTOM LOGIC app

>Implement logic for that field and publish it.

This is fully aligned with SAP Public Cloud Extensibility

Let's dive into the following steps:

 Create Custom Logic

> Navigate to Custom Logic Tile

Create a new implementation

  • You need to search for a business context. Here, we choose "Procurement: Purchase Requisition Item" 
  • Then select a correct extension point, click on step 2

  • Insert a suitable name  

  • Then click on review and create 
  • Once it is created, you have to publish it, then go to the Open Code Editor

Now you see the editor screen where you can write your logic.

Code: 

FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs> TYPE mmpur_s_pr_fldsel.

READ TABLE fieldselection_table ASSIGNING <fs> WITH KEY field = 'YY1_DEPARTMENT_PRI'.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
<fs>-fieldstatus = '+'.
ENDIF.

Save and publish it.

Before Implementing Custom Logic

 

After Implementing Custom Logic

Conclusion:

Custom Logic app is safe to use, and with minimum effort, we can create validation for any field in any standard app if it is there, and it is upgrade-safe too. SAP is also providing sample Code with it, so that it will help with the coding part. One more thing, we have to create an object using custom logic, in the customising server rather than the development server, then we move it using the "Export Software Collection" App. Even if the implementation is not present in the custom logic app, you can create a script for the validation, which we will discuss about it later.

Note: Before implementing, please go through the documentation provided by SAP for that particular extension.

Please feel free to connect if you have any queries 

Happy learning 😀

entry.title: Re: Post Refresh activities in People Anlaytics - 1H 2026
entry.content:
Hello @vtamil 

 

If by “refresh” you are referring to the SAP SuccessFactors Half-Yearly Production Release scheduled for May 15–16, there is no action required from your end. All release-related activities will be taken care of, and you should experience minimal to no disruption.

However, if you are referring to an Instance Refresh, please follow the guidelines outlined in 2315083 - Instance Refresh for Reporting and WFA Products (WFA/Canvas/Advanced Reporting/Story Reports) - SAP for Me

 

Thanks,

Raghu
